'''Cryon''' was a celestial body<ref name="Monsters">''[[Monsters and Aliens from George Lucas]]''</ref> located in the [[Cryon system/Legends|Cryon system]], a part of the [[Meram sector/Legends|Meram sector]]<ref name="EAOC" /> in the [[New Territories/Legends|New Territories]] portion of<ref name="TEA Cryon" /> the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]].<ref name="EAOC" />

==Behind the scenes==
Cryon was first mentioned in the [[1993]] book ''[[Monsters and Aliens from George Lucas]]'', a publication that combines creature designs and photographs from various projects of [[George Lucas]], creator of the ''[[Star Wars]]'' franchise, with original text by [[Bob Carrau]]. In the book, Cryon was introduced as a [[Planet/Legends|planet]] that was mentioned in a personal ad. In the ad, a [[Color/Legends|blue]]-[[Tongue/Legends|tongued]] self-described "Rubenesque beauty" was looking for a romantic encounter with a generous [[Gender/Legends|male]] from either Cryon or the planet [[Zorb]].<ref name="Monsters" />

[[Lucasfilm]] employee [[Leland Chee]], who maintains the [[Holocron continuity database]], has indicated that information contained in ''Monsters and Aliens from George Lucas'' is [[Canon|non-canonical]] with respect to the [[Star Wars Legends|''Star Wars'' Legends]] continuity.<ref name="Chee">{{SWBoards|url=thread.jspa?threadID=152583&start=690#14724917|thread=Holocron continuity database questions|user=[[Leland Chee|Leland Y Chee]]|date=2006-05-03|time=5:45 PM|quote=And is information from Monsters and Aliens from George Lucas canon? Unless referred to by a source that is in-continuity, the information is non-continuity.}}</ref> However, [[Jason Fry]],<ref name="Fry">{{Twitter|jasoncfry|status/294887358405410816|[[Jason Fry]]|quote=Pretty much." {{C|In response to "the systems in the Atlas's appendix are named after their most prominent planet/celestial body, correct?"}} and "planets not assigned a system in [a] published source can be assumed to reside in [the] system of [the] same name.|archivefile=File:JasonFryAtlas.png|archiveurl=https://archive.is/zThQV}}</ref> co-author of the [[2009]] reference book ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'',<ref name="TEA">''[[The Essential Atlas]]''</ref> has [[:File:JasonFryAtlas.png|stated]] that the [[Star system/Legends|star systems]] listed in the book's Appendix section are named after the most prominent planets or celestial bodies located in those systems. This article therefore considers the existence of Cryon to be canonical with respect to the Legends continuity.<ref name="Fry" /> ''The Essential Atlas'' placed the Cryon system, and therefore Cryon, in grid square O-4.<ref name="TEA Cryon" />
